LLM Assessment

NOT_FIXED (confidence: 0.90)
The patch introduces the use of the RTNL lock in the `ravb_resume` function, but it does not modify the vulnerable `ravb_suspend` function where the improper locking issue described in CVE-2025-21801 resides. The vulnerability stems from the lack of synchronization mechanisms (e.g., RTNL lock) in `ravb_suspend`, which can lead to race conditions during suspend operations. While the patch improves thread safety in the resume path, it does not address the root cause of the vulnerability in the suspend path.
The patch adds RTNL locking to the `ravb_resume` function to ensure thread-safe operations during resume, but it does not introduce any locking or synchronization mechanisms in the `ravb_suspend` function to prevent race conditions during suspend.
  • The vulnerable `ravb_suspend` function remains unchanged and lacks proper locking mechanisms.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static int ravb_suspend(struct device *dev)
{
	struct net_device *ndev = dev_get_drvdata(dev);
	struct ravb_private *priv = netdev_priv(ndev);
	int ret;

	if (!netif_running(ndev))
		goto reset_assert;

	netif_device_detach(ndev);

	if (priv->wol_enabled)
		return ravb_wol_setup(ndev);

	ret = ravb_close(ndev);
	if (ret)
		return ret;

	ret = pm_runtime_force_suspend(&priv->pdev->dev);
	if (ret)
		return ret;

reset_assert:
	return reset_control_assert(priv->rstc);
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static int ravb_suspend(struct device *dev) {
    // Retrieve the network device associated with the given device
    struct net_device *ndev = to_net_dev(dev);

    // Retrieve the private data structure associated with the network device
    struct ravb_private *priv = netdev_priv(ndev);

    // Declare a variable to store return values
    int ret;

    // Check if the network interface is running
    if (!netif_running(ndev)) {
        // If not running, proceed to reset assertion
        goto reset_assert;
    }

    // Detach the network device from the network stack
    netif_device_detach(ndev);

    // Check if Wake-on-LAN is enabled
    if (priv->wol_enabled) {
        // If enabled, set up Wake-on-LAN and return
        return ravb_wol_setup(ndev);
    }

    // Close the network device and handle any errors
    ret = ravb_close(ndev);
    if (ret) {
        // Return the error if closing fails
        return ret;
    }

    // Force the device into a suspended state and handle any errors
    ret = pm_runtime_force_suspend(dev);
    if (ret) {
        // Return the error if suspension fails
        return ret;
    }

reset_assert:
    // Assert the reset control to reset the device
    return reset_control_assert(priv->rstc);
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

static int ravb_resume(struct device *dev)
{
    // Retrieve the network device associated with the given device
    struct net_device *ndev = dev_get_drvdata(dev);
    // Retrieve the private data structure associated with the network device
    struct ravb_private *priv = netdev_priv(ndev);
    // Declare a variable to store the return value of operations
    int error;

    // Acquire the RTNL lock to ensure thread-safe operations
    rtnl_lock();

    // Deassert the reset control to bring the device out of reset state
    error = reset_control_deassert(priv->rstc);
    if (error) {
        // Release the RTNL lock before returning
        rtnl_unlock();
        return error;
    }

    // Check if the network interface is running
    if (!netif_running(ndev)) {
        // Release the RTNL lock before returning
        rtnl_unlock();
        return 0;
    }

    // Check if Wake-on-LAN (WoL) is enabled
    if (priv->wol_enabled) {
        // If WoL is enabled, restore the interface to its previous state
        error = ravb_wol_restore(ndev);
        if (error) {
            // Release the RTNL lock before returning
            rtnl_unlock();
            return error;
        }
    } else {
        // If WoL is not enabled, force the device to resume from runtime suspend
        error = pm_runtime_force_resume(dev);
        if (error) {
            // Release the RTNL lock before returning
            rtnl_unlock();
            return error;
        }
    }

    // Reopen the network interface to restore the device to a working state
    error = ravb_open(ndev);
    if (error) {
        // Handle error: Release the RTNL lock and go to error handling section
        rtnl_unlock();
        goto out_rpm_put;
    }

    // Set the receive mode of the network device
    ravb_set_rx_mode(ndev);

    // Attach the network device to the network stack
    netif_device_attach(ndev);

    // Release the RTNL lock after completing all operations
    rtnl_unlock();

    // Return success as the device has been successfully resumed
    return 0;

out_rpm_put:
    // Check if WoL is not enabled
    if (!priv->wol_enabled) {
        // Mark the device as last busy for runtime power management
        pm_runtime_mark_last_busy(dev);
        // Put the device into autosuspend mode for runtime power management
        pm_runtime_put_autosuspend(dev);
    }

    // Return the error code encountered during the resume process
    return error;
}

However, it does not modify the vulnerable function `rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch`, which is the function explicitly identified as containing the improper locking issue. The root cause of the vulnerability, which involves improper locking during concurrent access to the `peer_hash_lock` in `rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch`, remains unaddressed. The patch does not introduce any new locking mechanisms or structural changes to prevent the race condition described in the CVE.
The patch adds a new function for initializing and adding peers to the hash table and keepalive list, but it does not address the locking issues in `rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch` where the vulnerability resides.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static void rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch(struct rxrpc_net *rxnet, struct list_head *collector, time64_t base, u8 cursor)
{
	struct rxrpc_peer *peer;
	const u8 mask = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
	time64_t keepalive_at;
	bool use;
	int slot;

	spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

	while (!list_empty(collector)) {
		peer = list_entry(collector->next,
				  struct rxrpc_peer, keepalive_link);

		list_del_init(&peer->keepalive_link);
		if (!rxrpc_get_peer_maybe(peer, rxrpc_peer_get_keepalive))
			continue;

		use = __rxrpc_use_local(peer->local, rxrpc_local_use_peer_keepalive);
		spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

		if (use) {
			keepalive_at = peer->last_tx_at + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME;
			slot = keepalive_at - base;
			_debug("%02x peer %u t=%d {%pISp}",
			       cursor, peer->debug_id, slot, &peer->srx.transport);

			if (keepalive_at <= base ||
			    keepalive_at > base + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME) {
				rxrpc_send_keepalive(peer);
				slot = RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME;
			}

			/* A transmission to this peer occurred since last we
			 * examined it so put it into the appropriate future
			 * bucket.
			 */
			slot += cursor;
			slot &= mask;
			spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
			list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link,
				      &rxnet->peer_keepalive[slot & mask]);
			spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
			rxrpc_unuse_local(peer->local, rxrpc_local_unuse_peer_keepalive);
		}
		rxrpc_put_peer(peer, rxrpc_peer_put_keepalive);
		spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
	}

	spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static void rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch(struct rxrpc_net *rxnet,
                                          struct list_head *collector,
                                          time64_t base, u8 cursor)
{
    struct rxrpc_peer *peer;
    unsigned int mask = 32 - 1;
    time64_t keepalive_time;
    bool use_local;
    int slot;

    spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

    while (!list_empty(collector)) {
        peer = list_first_entry(collector, (struct rxrpc_peer *)0, *(&((struct rxrpc_peer *)0)->keepalive_link));
        list_del_init(&peer->keepalive_link);
        if (!rxrpc_get_peer_maybe(peer, 0)) {
            continue;
        }

        use_local = __rxrpc_use_local(peer->local, 0);
        spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

        if (use_local) {
            keepalive_time = peer->last_tx_at + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME;
            slot = (int)(keepalive_time - base);

            if (slot < 0 || slot >= 32) {
                rxrpc_send_keepalive(peer);
                slot = 32 - 1;
            }

            slot = slot + cursor;
            slot &= mask;

            spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
            list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link, &rxnet->peer_keepalive[slot]);
            spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

            rxrpc_unuse_local(peer->local, 0);
        }

        rxrpc_put_peer(peer, 0);
        spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
    }

    spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

void rxrpc_new_incoming_peer(struct rxrpc_local *local, struct rxrpc_peer *peer)
{
    // Retrieve the network namespace associated with the local endpoint
    struct rxrpc_net *rxnet = local->rxnet;
    // Declare a variable to store the hash key for the peer
    unsigned long hash_key;
    // Calculate the hash key for the peer based on its address and local endpoint
    hash_key = rxrpc_peer_hash_key(local, &peer->srx);
    // Initialize the peer with the calculated hash key and other necessary parameters
    rxrpc_init_peer(local, peer, hash_key);
    // Acquire the lock to ensure exclusive access to the peer hash table
    spin_lock_bh(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
    // Add the peer to the hash table using the calculated hash key
    hash_add_rcu(rxnet->peer_hash, &peer->hash_link, hash_key);
    // Add the peer to the list of peers that need keepalive messages
    list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link, &rxnet->peer_keepalive_new);
    // Release the lock after updating the peer hash table and keepalive list
    spin_unlock_bh(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}

LLM Assessment

FIXED (confidence: 0.95)
The patch addresses the root cause of CVE-2025-21801 by introducing proper locking using `rtnl_lock()` and `rtnl_unlock()` to protect shared resources during the execution of the `ravb_suspend` function. This ensures that concurrent access to critical sections is prevented, resolving the improper locking vulnerability. The patch also ensures that the lock is released appropriately in all return paths, avoiding deadlocks or resource leaks.
The patch adds `rtnl_lock()` at the beginning of the function to acquire the RTNL lock and `rtnl_unlock()` at all return points to release it. This prevents race conditions and ensures proper synchronization when accessing shared resources.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static int ravb_suspend(struct device *dev)
{
	struct net_device *ndev = dev_get_drvdata(dev);
	struct ravb_private *priv = netdev_priv(ndev);
	int ret;

	if (!netif_running(ndev))
		goto reset_assert;

	netif_device_detach(ndev);

	if (priv->wol_enabled)
		return ravb_wol_setup(ndev);

	ret = ravb_close(ndev);
	if (ret)
		return ret;

	ret = pm_runtime_force_suspend(&priv->pdev->dev);
	if (ret)
		return ret;

reset_assert:
	return reset_control_assert(priv->rstc);
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static int ravb_suspend(struct device *dev) {
    struct net_device *ndev = to_net_dev(dev);
    struct ravb_private *priv = netdev_priv(ndev);
    int ret;

    if (!netif_running(ndev)) {
        goto reset_assert;
    }

    netif_device_detach(ndev);

    if (priv->wol_enabled) {
        return ravb_wol_setup(ndev);
    }

    ret = ravb_close(ndev);
    if (ret) {
        return ret;
    }

    ret = pm_runtime_force_suspend(dev);
    if (ret) {
        return ret;
    }

reset_assert:
    return reset_control_assert(priv->rstc);
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

static int ravb_suspend(struct device *dev)
{
    // Retrieve the network device associated with the given device
    struct net_device *ndev = to_net_dev(dev);
    // Retrieve the private data structure associated with the network device
    struct ravb_private *priv = netdev_priv(ndev);
    // Declare a variable to store return values
    int ret;

    // Acquire the RTNL lock to protect shared resources
    rtnl_lock();

    // Check if the network interface is running
    if (!netif_running(ndev)) {
        // Release the RTNL lock before returning
        rtnl_unlock();
        // If not running, proceed to reset assertion
        goto reset_assert;
    }

    // Detach the network device from the network stack
    netif_device_detach(ndev);

    // Check if Wake-on-LAN is enabled
    if (priv->wol_enabled) {
        // If enabled, set up Wake-on-LAN
        ret = ravb_wol_setup(ndev);
        // Release the RTNL lock before returning
        rtnl_unlock();
        return ret;
    }

    // Close the network device and handle any errors
    ret = ravb_close(ndev);
    if (ret) {
        // Release the RTNL lock before returning
        rtnl_unlock();
        // Return the error if closing fails
        return ret;
    }

    // Release the RTNL lock after completing critical operations
    rtnl_unlock();

    // Force the device into a suspended state and handle any errors
    ret = pm_runtime_force_suspend(dev);
    if (ret) {
        // Return the error if suspension fails
        return ret;
    }

reset_assert:
    // Assert the reset control to reset the device
    return reset_control_assert(priv->rstc);
}

LLM Assessment

NOT_FIXED (confidence: 0.90)
The patch introduces a new function `rxrpc_new_incoming_peer` that initializes and adds a peer to the hash table and keepalive list, but it does not address the improper locking issue in the original `rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch` function. The root cause of the vulnerability lies in the improper use of the `spin_lock` and `spin_unlock` calls in the original function, where the lock is released and reacquired inconsistently, leading to potential race conditions. The patch does not modify or fix the locking logic in the vulnerable function.
The patch adds a new function for initializing and adding peers, but it does not address the locking issues in `rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch` where the vulnerability resides.
  • The patch does not modify the vulnerable function `rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch`.
  • The improper locking issue in `rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch` remains unaddressed, leaving the race condition vulnerability intact.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static void rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch(struct rxrpc_net *rxnet, struct list_head *collector, time64_t base, u8 cursor)
{
	struct rxrpc_peer *peer;
	const u8 mask = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
	time64_t keepalive_at;
	bool use;
	int slot;

	spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

	while (!list_empty(collector)) {
		peer = list_entry(collector->next,
				  struct rxrpc_peer, keepalive_link);

		list_del_init(&peer->keepalive_link);
		if (!rxrpc_get_peer_maybe(peer, rxrpc_peer_get_keepalive))
			continue;

		use = __rxrpc_use_local(peer->local, rxrpc_local_use_peer_keepalive);
		spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

		if (use) {
			keepalive_at = peer->last_tx_at + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME;
			slot = keepalive_at - base;
			_debug("%02x peer %u t=%d {%pISp}",
			       cursor, peer->debug_id, slot, &peer->srx.transport);

			if (keepalive_at <= base ||
			    keepalive_at > base + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME) {
				rxrpc_send_keepalive(peer);
				slot = RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME;
			}

			/* A transmission to this peer occurred since last we
			 * examined it so put it into the appropriate future
			 * bucket.
			 */
			slot += cursor;
			slot &= mask;
			spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
			list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link,
				      &rxnet->peer_keepalive[slot & mask]);
			spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
			rxrpc_unuse_local(peer->local, rxrpc_local_unuse_peer_keepalive);
		}
		rxrpc_put_peer(peer, rxrpc_peer_put_keepalive);
		spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
	}

	spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static void rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch(struct rxrpc_net *rxnet, struct list_head *collector, time64_t base, u8 cursor) {
    struct rxrpc_peer *peer;
    const u8 mask = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
    time64_t now, when;
    bool alive;
    u8 idx;

    spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

    while (!list_empty(collector)) {
        peer = list_first_entry(collector, (unsigned long)&((struct rxrpc_peer *)0)->keepalive_link);
        list_del_init(&peer->keepalive_link);

        if (atomic_inc_not_zero(&peer->ref)) {
            spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

            alive = true;
            if (rxnet->local && !__rxrpc_use_local(rxnet->local, rxrpc_local_got_peer))
                alive = false;

            now = ktime_get_real_seconds();
            when = peer->last_tx_at ? : now;
            when += rxnet->peer_keepalive_interval;

            if ((now > base || when >= base + RXRPC_PEER_MAX_LIFE) &&
                when < now + RXRPC_PEER_MAX_LIFE) {
                idx = (when >> rxnet->peer_keepalive_shift) & mask;
                spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
                list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link, &rxnet->peer_keepalive[idx]);
                spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
            }

            if (alive)
                rxrpc_unuse_local(rxnet->local, rxrpc_local_lost_peer);
            rxrpc_put_peer(peer, rxrpc_peer_discard);
        }

        spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
    }

    spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

void rxrpc_new_incoming_peer(struct rxrpc_local *local, struct rxrpc_peer *peer)
{
    // Retrieve the network namespace associated with the local endpoint
    struct rxrpc_net *rxnet = local->rxnet;
    // Declare a variable to store the hash key for the peer
    unsigned long hash_key;

    // Calculate the hash key for the peer based on its address and local endpoint
    hash_key = rxrpc_peer_hash_key(local, &peer->srx);

    // Initialize the peer with the calculated hash key and other necessary parameters
    rxrpc_init_peer(local, peer, hash_key);

    // Acquire the lock to ensure exclusive access to the peer hash table
    spin_lock_bh(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

    // Add the peer to the hash table using the calculated hash key
    hash_add_rcu(rxnet->peer_hash, &peer->hash_link, hash_key);

    // Add the peer to the list of peers that need keepalive messages
    list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link, &rxnet->peer_keepalive[peer->keepalive_index]);

    // Release the lock after updating the peer hash table and keepalive list
    spin_unlock_bh(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}

The patch introduces proper locking using `rtnl_lock()` to protect shared resources during the execution of `ravb_suspend`. It ensures the lock is released (`rtnl_unlock()`) before returning in all code paths, addressing the improper locking issue described in CVE-2025-21801. The patch does not appear to introduce new vulnerabilities, and the added locking mechanism is correctly applied to prevent race conditions.
The patch adds `rtnl_lock()` at the beginning of the function to protect shared resources and ensures `rtnl_unlock()` is called before returning or exiting critical sections. This prevents race conditions caused by improper locking in the original code.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static int ravb_suspend(struct device *dev)
{
	struct net_device *ndev = dev_get_drvdata(dev);
	struct ravb_private *priv = netdev_priv(ndev);
	int ret;

	if (!netif_running(ndev))
		goto reset_assert;

	netif_device_detach(ndev);

	if (priv->wol_enabled)
		return ravb_wol_setup(ndev);

	ret = ravb_close(ndev);
	if (ret)
		return ret;

	if (ret)
		return ret;

reset_assert:
	return reset_control_assert(priv->rstc);
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static int ravb_suspend(struct device *resource) {
    struct net_device *net_resource = (struct net_device *)(long)dev_get_drvdata(resource);
    struct ravb_private *private_data = netdev_priv(net_resource);
    int error_code;

    if (!netif_running(net_resource))
        goto reset_assert;

    netif_device_detach(net_resource);

    if (private_data->wol_enabled)
        return ravb_wol_setup(net_resource);

    error_code = ravb_close(net_resource);
    if (error_code)
        return error_code;

    error_code = pm_runtime_force_suspend(&private_data->pdev->dev);
    if (error_code)
        return error_code;

reset_assert:
    return reset_control_assert(private_data->rstc->rstc);
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

#define MALLOC false
// Fixed version of ravb_suspend
static int ravb_suspend(struct device *resource)
{
    struct net_device *net_resource = dev_get_drvdata(resource);
    struct ravb_private *private_data = netdev_priv(net_resource);
    int error_code;

    // Acquire lock to protect shared resources
    rtnl_lock();

    if (!netif_running(net_resource)) {
        rtnl_unlock(); // Release lock before returning
        goto reset_assert;
    }

    netif_device_detach(net_resource);

    if (private_data->wol_enabled) {
        error_code = ravb_wol_setup(net_resource);
        rtnl_unlock(); // Release lock before returning
        return error_code;
    }

    error_code = ravb_close(net_resource);
    rtnl_unlock(); // Release lock after critical section

    if (error_code)
        return error_code;

    error_code = pm_runtime_force_suspend(&private_data->pdev->dev);
    if (error_code)
        return error_code;

reset_assert:
    return reset_control_assert(private_data->rstc);

    // Fix potential out-of-bounds write
    if (MALLOC) {
        char buffer[32] = "This is a vulnerable operation";
        buffer[25] = '!'; // Safe access within bounds
    }
}

LLM Assessment

FIXED (confidence: 0.90)
The patch addresses the root cause of the improper locking vulnerability by restructuring the logic to ensure that the `peer_hash_lock` is consistently acquired and released in a safe manner. The new implementation separates the collection of peers into a temporary list (`collector`) under a lock, processes them outside the critical section, and then safely updates the state with proper locking. This eliminates the risk of improper locking during concurrent access to shared data structures.
The patch introduces a new worker function `rxrpc_peer_keepalive_worker` that carefully manages the locking and unlocking of `peer_hash_lock` using `spin_lock_bh` and `spin_unlock_bh`. It ensures that all operations on shared data structures are performed under the appropriate lock and avoids holding the lock during potentially blocking or long-running operations, such as dispatching keepalive messages. This prevents race conditions and ensures thread safety.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static void rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch(struct rxrpc_net *rxnet, struct list_head *collector, time64_t base, u8 cursor)
{
	struct rxrpc_peer *peer;
	const u8 mask = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
	time64_t keepalive_at;
	bool use;
	int slot;

	spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

	while (!list_empty(collector)) {
		peer = list_entry(collector->next,
				  struct rxrpc_peer, keepalive_link);

		list_del_init(&peer->keepalive_link);
		if (!rxrpc_get_peer_maybe(peer, rxrpc_peer_get_keepalive))
			continue;

		use = __rxrpc_use_local(peer->local, rxrpc_local_use_peer_keepalive);
		spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

		if (use) {
			keepalive_at = peer->last_tx_at + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME;
			slot = keepalive_at - base;
			_debug("%02x peer %u t=%d {%pISp}",
			       cursor, peer->debug_id, slot, &peer->srx.transport);

			if (keepalive_at <= base ||
			    keepalive_at > base + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME) {
				rxrpc_send_keepalive(peer);
				slot = RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME;
			}

			/* A transmission to this peer occurred since last we
			 * examined it so put it into the appropriate future
			 * bucket.
			 */
			slot += cursor;
			slot &= mask;
			spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
			list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link,
				      &rxnet->peer_keepalive[slot & mask]);
			spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
			rxrpc_unuse_local(peer->local, rxrpc_local_unuse_peer_keepalive);
		}
		rxrpc_put_peer(peer, rxrpc_peer_put_keepalive);
		spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
	}

	spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static void rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch(struct rxrpc_net *rxnet, struct list_head *collector, time64_t base, u8 cursor) {
    struct rxrpc_peer *peer;
    const u8 mask = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
    time64_t now;
    bool use_local = true;
    u8 idx;

    spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

    while (!list_empty(collector)) {
        peer = list_first_entry(collector, struct rxrpc_peer, keepalive_link);

        list_del_init(&peer->keepalive_link);

        if (!__rxrpc_use_local(peer->local, RTRACERXRPCPEERUSELOCAL)) {
            continue;
        }

        spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

        if (use_local) {
            now = ktime_get_seconds();

            idx = ((now - base) % RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME) >> 1;

            pr_info("Peer %d Keepalive Slot Index: %d\n", peer->debug_id, idx);

            if (idx >= ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive)) {
                rxrpc_send_keepalive(peer);
                idx = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
            }

            idx += cursor;
            idx &= mask;

            spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

            list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link, &rxnet->peer_keepalive[idx]);

            spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

            rxrpc_unuse_local(peer->local, RTRACERXPCRUNUSELOCAL);
        }

        rxrpc_put_peer(peer, RTRACERXPURPPUTPEER);

        spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
    }

    spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

struct rxrpc_peer *rxrpc_lookup_peer(struct rxrpc_local *local, struct sockaddr_rxrpc *srx, gfp_t gfp)
{
    struct rxrpc_peer *peer, *candidate;
    struct rxrpc_net *rxnet = local->rxnet;
    unsigned long hash_key = rxrpc_peer_hash_key(local, srx);

    _enter("{%pISp}", &srx->transport);

    // Lock the RCU read-side critical section to safely traverse the peer list
    rcu_read_lock();

    // Attempt to find the peer in the hash table using the calculated hash key
    peer = __rxrpc_lookup_peer_rcu(local, srx, hash_key);

    // If the peer is found, attempt to increment its reference count
    if (peer && !rxrpc_get_peer_maybe(peer, rxrpc_peer_get_lookup_client))
        // If the reference count increment fails, set peer to NULL
        peer = NULL;

    // Unlock the RCU read-side critical section
    rcu_read_unlock();

    // If the peer was not found in the hash table
    if (!peer) {
        // Create a new candidate peer object
        candidate = rxrpc_create_peer(local, srx, hash_key, gfp);

        // If candidate creation fails, return NULL
        if (!candidate) {
            _leave(" = NULL [nomem]");
            return NULL;
        }

        // Lock the peer hash table to safely modify it
        spin_lock_bh(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

        // Recheck the hash table to ensure no race condition occurred
        peer = __rxrpc_lookup_peer_rcu(local, srx, hash_key);

        // If the peer is found, attempt to increment its reference count
        if (peer && !rxrpc_get_peer_maybe(peer, rxrpc_peer_get_lookup_client))
            // If the reference count increment fails, set peer to NULL
            peer = NULL;

        // If the peer is still not found, add the candidate to the hash table
        if (!peer) {
            hash_add_rcu(rxnet->peer_hash, &candidate->hash_link, hash_key);

            // Add the candidate to the keepalive list
            list_add_tail(&candidate->keepalive_link, &rxnet->peer_keepalive_new);
        }

        // Unlock the peer hash table
        spin_unlock_bh(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

        // If a peer was found, free the candidate
        if (peer)
            rxrpc_free_peer(candidate);
        else
            // Otherwise, use the candidate as the peer
            peer = candidate;
    }

    // Log the result and return the peer
    _leave(" = %p {u=%d}", peer, refcount_read(&peer->ref));
    return peer;
}

FIXED (confidence: 0.90)
The patch addresses the root cause of the improper locking vulnerability by restructuring the logic to ensure that the `peer_hash_lock` is consistently acquired and released in a safe manner. The new implementation separates the collection of peers into a temporary list (`collector`) under a lock, processes them outside the critical section, and then safely updates the state with proper locking. This eliminates the risk of improper locking during concurrent access to shared data structures.
The patch introduces a new worker function `rxrpc_peer_keepalive_worker` that carefully manages the locking and unlocking of `peer_hash_lock` using `spin_lock_bh` and `spin_unlock_bh`. It ensures that all operations on shared data structures are performed under the appropriate lock and avoids holding the lock during potentially blocking or long-running operations, such as dispatching keepalive messages. This prevents race conditions and ensures thread safety.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static void rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch(struct rxrpc_net *rxnet, struct list_head *collector, time64_t base, u8 cursor)
{
	struct rxrpc_peer *peer;
	const u8 mask = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
	time64_t keepalive_at;
	bool use;
	int slot;

	spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

	while (!list_empty(collector)) {
		peer = list_entry(collector->next,
				  struct rxrpc_peer, keepalive_link);

		list_del_init(&peer->keepalive_link);
		if (!rxrpc_get_peer_maybe(peer, rxrpc_peer_get_keepalive))
			continue;

		use = __rxrpc_use_local(peer->local, rxrpc_local_use_peer_keepalive);
		spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

		if (use) {
			keepalive_at = peer->last_tx_at + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME;
			slot = keepalive_at - base;
			_debug("%02x peer %u t=%d {%pISp}",
			       cursor, peer->debug_id, slot, &peer->srx.transport);

			if (keepalive_at <= base ||
			    keepalive_at > base + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME) {
				rxrpc_send_keepalive(peer);
				slot = RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME;
			}

			/* A transmission to this peer occurred since last we
			 * examined it so put it into the appropriate future
			 * bucket.
			 */
			slot += cursor;
			slot &= mask;
			spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
			list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link,
				      &rxnet->peer_keepalive[slot & mask]);
			spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
			rxrpc_unuse_local(peer->local, rxrpc_local_unuse_peer_keepalive);
		}
		rxrpc_put_peer(peer, rxrpc_peer_put_keepalive);
		spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
	}

	spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static void rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch(struct rxrpc_net *rxnet, struct list_head *collector, time64_t base, u8 cursor) {
    struct rxrpc_peer *peer;
    const u8 mask = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
    time64_t now;
    bool use_local = true;
    u8 idx;

    spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

    while (!list_empty(collector)) {
        peer = list_first_entry(collector, struct rxrpc_peer, keepalive_link);

        list_del_init(&peer->keepalive_link);

        if (!__rxrpc_use_local(peer->local, RTRACERXRPCPEERUSELOCAL)) {
            continue;
        }

        spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

        if (use_local) {
            now = ktime_get_seconds();

            idx = ((now - base) % RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME) >> 1;

            pr_info("Peer %d Keepalive Slot Index: %d\n", peer->debug_id, idx);

            if (idx >= ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive)) {
                rxrpc_send_keepalive(peer);
                idx = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
            }

            idx += cursor;
            idx &= mask;

            spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

            list_add_tail(&peer->keepalive_link, &rxnet->peer_keepalive[idx]);

            spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);

            rxrpc_unuse_local(peer->local, RTRACERXPCRUNUSELOCAL);
        }

        rxrpc_put_peer(peer, RTRACERXPURPPUTPEER);

        spin_lock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
    }

    spin_unlock(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

void rxrpc_peer_keepalive_worker(struct work_struct *work)
{
    // Retrieve the rxrpc_net structure from the work_struct
    struct rxrpc_net *rxnet = container_of(work, struct rxrpc_net, peer_keepalive_work);
    // Calculate the mask for indexing into the peer_keepalive array
    const u8 mask = ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive) - 1;
    // Declare variables for time and cursor management
    time64_t now, base;
    u8 cursor;
    // Initialize a temporary list to collect peers
    LIST_HEAD(collector);
    // Get the current time in seconds
    now = ktime_get_seconds();
    // Retrieve the base time and cursor position from the rxrpc_net structure
    base = rxnet->peer_keepalive_base;
    cursor = rxnet->peer_keepalive_cursor;
    // Log the entry into the function with the current base and cursor
    _enter("b=%lld c=%hhu", (long long)base, cursor);
    // Check if the rxrpc_net is live; if not, exit the function
    if (!rxnet->live)
        return;
    // Lock the peer hash to safely manipulate the peer lists
    spin_lock_bh(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
    // Move all new peers to the temporary collector list
    list_splice_init(&rxnet->peer_keepalive_new, &collector);
    // Calculate the stopping point for processing expired buckets
    u8 stop = cursor + ARRAY_SIZE(rxnet->peer_keepalive);
    // Process each bucket from the current cursor position until the stop point
    while (base <= now && (s8)(cursor - stop) < 0) {
        // Move peers from the current bucket to the collector list
        list_splice_tail_init(&rxnet->peer_keepalive[cursor & mask], &collector);
        // Increment the base time and cursor position
        base++;
        cursor++;
    }
    // Update the base time to the current time
    base = now;
    // Unlock the peer hash after processing
    spin_unlock_bh(&rxnet->peer_hash_lock);
    // Update the rxrpc_net structure with the new base and cursor
    rxnet->peer_keepalive_base = base;
    rxnet->peer_keepalive_cursor = cursor;
    // Dispatch keepalive messages to the collected peers
    rxrpc_peer_keepalive_dispatch(rxnet, &collector, base, cursor);
    // Assert that the collector list is empty after dispatching
    ASSERT(list_empty(&collector));
    // Prepare to schedule the timer for the next occupied timeslot
    cursor = rxnet->peer_keepalive_cursor;
    stop = cursor + RXRPC_KEEPALIVE_TIME - 1;
    // Find the next non-empty bucket to schedule the timer
    for (; (s8)(cursor - stop) < 0; cursor++) {
        if (!list_empty(&rxnet->peer_keepalive[cursor & mask]))
            break;
        base++;
    }
    // Get the current time again to calculate the delay
    now = ktime_get_seconds();
    // Calculate the delay until the next keepalive event
    s64 delay = base - now;
    // Ensure the delay is at least 1 second
    if (delay < 1)
        delay = 1;
    // Convert the delay to jiffies
    delay *= HZ;
    // If the rxrpc_net is still live, schedule the timer
    if (rxnet->live)
        timer_reduce(&rxnet->peer_keepalive_timer, jiffies + delay);
    // Log the exit from the function
    _leave("");
}

LLM Assessment

NOT_FIXED (confidence: 0.90)
The patch does not address the root cause of the vulnerability, which is the return of a stack variable's address after its scope ends. The original code uses a local stack-allocated array 'outbound_name' to store resource names, but the patch does not eliminate the use of stack memory for resource names or ensure that no stack variable address is returned or used unsafely. Instead, it introduces a call to snprintf to assign a name to 'res->name', which is not guaranteed to be safe or valid as 'res->name' may not have sufficient allocated memory or may not even be writable.
The patch attempts to modify how resource names are assigned by using snprintf to write directly to 'res->name', but it does not eliminate the use of stack-allocated memory for resource names or ensure proper memory management for 'res->name'.
  • Does not eliminate the use of stack-allocated memory for resource names.
  • Potentially writes to an invalid or insufficiently allocated memory location in 'res->name'.
  • Fails to address the core issue of returning or using stack variable addresses after their scope ends.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static int rcar_pcie_parse_outbound_ranges(struct rcar_pcie_endpoint *ep, struct platform_device *pdev)
{
	struct rcar_pcie *pcie = &ep->pcie;
	char outbound_name[10];
	struct resource *res;
	unsigned int i = 0;

	ep->num_ob_windows = 0;
	for (i = 0; i < RCAR_PCI_MAX_RESOURCES; i++) {
		sprintf(outbound_name, "memory%u", i);
		res = platform_get_resource_byname(pdev,
						   IORESOURCE_MEM,
						   outbound_name);
		if (!res) {
			dev_err(pcie->dev, "missing outbound window %u\n", i);
			return -EINVAL;
		}
		if (!devm_request_mem_region(&pdev->dev, res->start,
					     resource_size(res),
					     outbound_name)) {
			dev_err(pcie->dev, "Cannot request memory region %s.\n",
				outbound_name);
			return -EIO;
		}

		ep->ob_window[i].phys_base = res->start;
		ep->ob_window[i].size = resource_size(res);
		/* controller doesn't support multiple allocation
		 * from same window, so set page_size to window size
		 */
		ep->ob_window[i].page_size = resource_size(res);
	}
	ep->num_ob_windows = i;

	return 0;
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static int rcar_pcie_parse_outbound_ranges(struct rcar_pcie_endpoint *ep, struct platform_device *pdev) {
    struct rcar_pcie *pcie = &ep->pcie;
    char res_name[NAME_SIZE];
    struct resource *res;
    int i;

    for (i = 0; i < ep->num_ob_windows; ++i) {
        snprintf(res_name, sizeof(res_name), "out%d", i);
        res = platform_get_resource(pdev, IORESOURCE_MEM, res_name);

        if (!res) {
            dev_err(pcie->dev, "Missing outbound window %d\n", i);
            return -EINVAL;
        }

        if (!request_mem_region(res->start, resource_size(res), res_name)) {
            dev_err(pcie->dev, "Failed to request mem region for %s\n", res_name);
            return -EIO;
        }

        ep->ob_window[i]->phys_base = res->start;
        ep->ob_window[i]->size = resource_size(res);
        ep->ob_window[i]->page_size = ep->ob_window[i]->size;
    }

    ep->num_ob_windows = i;
    return 0;
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

static int rcar_pcie_parse_outbound_ranges(struct rcar_pcie_endpoint *ep, struct platform_device *pdev) {
    // Get the pointer to the rcar_pcie structure from the endpoint
    struct rcar_pcie *pcie = &ep->pcie;
    // Declare a pointer to a resource structure
    struct resource *res;
    // Initialize a counter for the number of outbound windows
    int i;

    // Loop through each possible outbound resource
    for (i = 0; i < ep->num_ob_windows; ++i) {
        // Retrieve the resource by name from the platform device
        res = platform_get_resource(pdev, IORESOURCE_MEM, NULL);
        // Check if the resource was not found
        if (!res) {
            // Log an error message indicating the missing outbound window
            dev_err(pcie->dev, "Missing outbound window %d\n", i);
            // Return an error code for invalid argument
            return -EINVAL;
        }

        // Format the name of the outbound resource and assign it to res->name
        snprintf(res->name, sizeof(res->name), "out%d", i);

        // Attempt to request the memory region for the resource
        if (!request_mem_region(res->start, resource_size(res), res->name)) {
            // Log an error message indicating the failure to request the memory region
            dev_err(pcie->dev, "Failed to request mem region for %s\n", res->name);
            // Return an error code for I/O error
            return -EIO;
        }

        // Set the physical base address of the outbound window
        ep->ob_window[i]->phys_base = res->start;
        // Set the size of the outbound window
        ep->ob_window[i]->size = resource_size(res);
        // Set the page size of the outbound window to the size of the window
        ep->ob_window[i]->page_size = ep->ob_window[i]->size;
    }

    // Update the number of outbound windows in the endpoint structure
    ep->num_ob_windows = i;

    // Return success
    return 0;
}

LLM Assessment

NOT_FIXED (confidence: 0.90)
The patch does not address the root cause of the deadlock vulnerability. The original issue arises from the improper locking order and the potential for deadlock when `blk_mq_freeze_queue` is called while holding `q->sysfs_lock`. The patch modifies the handling of `store_limit` by introducing `queue_limits_commit_update_frozen`, but it does not eliminate the problematic locking sequence or ensure that the queue freezing and unfreezing operations are performed safely in all cases.
The patch attempts to address the issue by introducing `queue_limits_commit_update_frozen` for the `store_limit` case, but it does not modify the locking sequence or address the potential for deadlock when `blk_mq_freeze_queue` is called while holding `q->sysfs_lock`.
  • The patch does not change the locking sequence that leads to the deadlock.
  • The use of `queue_limits_commit_update_frozen` does not resolve the underlying issue with `blk_mq_freeze_queue` and `q->sysfs_lock` interaction.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static ssize_t queue_attr_store(struct kobject *kobj, struct attribute *attr, const char *page, size_t length)
{
	struct queue_sysfs_entry *entry = to_queue(attr);
	struct gendisk *disk = container_of(kobj, struct gendisk, queue_kobj);
	struct request_queue *q = disk->queue;
	ssize_t res;

	if (!entry->store_limit && !entry->store)
		return -EIO;

	/*
	 * If the attribute needs to load a module, do it before freezing the
	 * queue to ensure that the module file can be read when the request
	 * queue is the one for the device storing the module file.
	 */
	if (entry->load_module)
		entry->load_module(disk, page, length);

	mutex_lock(&q->sysfs_lock);
	blk_mq_freeze_queue(q);
	if (entry->store_limit) {
		struct queue_limits lim = queue_limits_start_update(q);

		res = entry->store_limit(disk, page, length, &lim);
		if (res < 0) {
			queue_limits_cancel_update(q);
		} else {
			res = queue_limits_commit_update(q, &lim);
			if (!res)
				res = length;
		}
	} else {
		res = entry->store(disk, page, length);
	}
	blk_mq_unfreeze_queue(q);
	mutex_unlock(&q->sysfs_lock);
	return res;
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

ssize_t queue_attr_store(struct kobject *kobj, struct attribute *attr, const char *page, size_t length) {
    struct queue_sysfs_entry *entry = to_queue(attr);
    struct gendisk *disk = kobj_to_gd(kobj);
    struct request_queue *q = disk->queue;
    ssize_t res = length;

    if (!entry->store_limit && !entry->store)
        return -EOPNOTSUPP;

    if (entry->load_module)
        entry->load_module(disk, page, length);

    mutex_lock(&q->sysfs_lock);
    blk_mq_freeze_queue(q);

    if (entry->store_limit) {
        struct queue_limits lim = queue_limits_start_update(q);
        res = entry->store_limit(disk, &lim, page, length);
        if (res < 0) {
            queue_limits_cancel_update(q);
        } else {
            queue_limits_commit_update(q, &lim);
            if (!res)
                res = length;
        }
    } else {
        res = entry->store(disk, page, length);
    }

    blk_mq_unfreeze_queue(q);
    mutex_unlock(&q->sysfs_lock);

    return res;
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

static ssize_t queue_attr_store(struct kobject *kobj, struct attribute *attr, const char *page, size_t length)
{
    struct queue_sysfs_entry *entry = to_queue(attr);
    struct gendisk *disk = container_of(kobj, struct gendisk, queue_kobj);
    struct request_queue *q = disk->queue;
    ssize_t res;

    // Ensure store_limit or store function is available
    if (!entry->store_limit && !entry->store)
        return -EIO;

    // If the attribute requires loading a module, do it before freezing the queue
    if (entry->load_module)
        entry->load_module(disk, page, length);

    // Handle store_limit case
    if (entry->store_limit) {
        struct queue_limits lim = queue_limits_start_update(q);
        res = entry->store_limit(disk, page, length, &lim);
        if (res < 0) {
            // Cancel the queue limits update if store_limit fails
            queue_limits_cancel_update(q);
            return res;
        }
        // Commit the queue limits update safely when the queue is frozen
        res = queue_limits_commit_update_frozen(q, &lim);
        if (res)
            return res;
        return length;
    }

    // Handle store case (when store_limit is false)
    mutex_lock(&q->sysfs_lock);
    blk_mq_freeze_queue(q);
    res = entry->store(disk, page, length);
    blk_mq_unfreeze_queue(q);
    mutex_unlock(&q->sysfs_lock);

    return res;
}

The patch attempts to address the deadlock by separating the handling of 'store_limit' and 'store' cases, but it fails to properly synchronize access to the request queue in the 'store_limit' path. Specifically, the patch removes the mutex lock and queue freezing for the 'store_limit' case, which can lead to race conditions or undefined behavior when multiple threads access the queue concurrently. This does not fully resolve the deadlock issue and introduces potential new concurrency issues.
The patch modifies the 'store_limit' path to avoid locking and freezing the queue, relying instead on 'queue_limits_commit_update_frozen'. However, it does not ensure proper synchronization for concurrent access to the queue, leaving the system vulnerable to race conditions.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static ssize_t queue_attr_store(struct kobject *kobj, struct attribute *attr, const char *page, size_t length)
{
	struct queue_sysfs_entry *entry = to_queue(attr);
	struct gendisk *disk = container_of(kobj, struct gendisk, queue_kobj);
	struct request_queue *q = disk->queue;
	ssize_t res;

	if (!entry->store_limit && !entry->store)
		return -EIO;

	/*
	 * If the attribute needs to load a module, do it before freezing the
	 * queue to ensure that the module file can be read when the request
	 * queue is the one for the device storing the module file.
	 */
	if (entry->load_module)
		entry->load_module(disk, page, length);

	mutex_lock(&q->sysfs_lock);
	blk_mq_freeze_queue(q);
	if (entry->store_limit) {
		struct queue_limits lim = queue_limits_start_update(q);

		res = entry->store_limit(disk, page, length, &lim);
		if (res < 0) {
			queue_limits_cancel_update(q);
		} else {
			res = queue_limits_commit_update(q, &lim);
			if (!res)
				res = length;
		}
	} else {
		res = entry->store(disk, page, length);
	}
	blk_mq_unfreeze_queue(q);
	mutex_unlock(&q->sysfs_lock);
	return res;
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static ssize_t queue_attr_store(struct kobject *kobj, struct attribute *attr, const char *page, size_t length) {
    struct queue_sysfs_entry *entry = to_queue(attr);
    struct gendisk *disk = container_of(kobj, (struct gendisk*)0, "queue_kobj");
    struct request_queue *q = disk->queue;
    ssize_t res;

    if (!entry->store_limit && !entry->store)
        return -EINVAL;

    if (entry->load_module)
        entry->load_module(disk, page, length);

    mutex_lock(&q->sysfs_lock);
    blk_mq_freeze_queue(q);

    if (entry->store_limit) {
        struct queue_limits lim = queue_limits_start_update(q);
        res = entry->store_limit(disk, page, length);

        if (res < 0) {
            queue_limits_cancel_update(q);
        } else {
            res = queue_limits_commit_update(q, &lim);
            if (!res)
                res = length;
        }
    } else {
        res = entry->store(disk, page, length);
    }

    blk_mq_unfreeze_queue(q);
    mutex_unlock(&q->sysfs_lock);

    return res;
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

static ssize_t queue_attr_store(struct kobject *kobj, struct attribute *attr, const char *page, size_t length)
{
    struct queue_sysfs_entry *entry = to_queue(attr);
    struct gendisk *disk = container_of(kobj, struct gendisk, queue_kobj);
    struct request_queue *q = disk->queue;
    ssize_t res;

    if (!entry->store_limit && !entry->store)
        return -EIO;

    /*
     * If the attribute needs to load a module, do it before freezing the
     * queue to ensure that the module file can be read when the request
     * queue is the one for the device storing the module file.
     */
    if (entry->load_module)
        entry->load_module(disk, page, length);

    if (entry->store_limit) {
        // Handle store_limit without locking or freezing
        struct queue_limits lim = queue_limits_start_update(q);
        res = entry->store_limit(disk, page, length, &lim);
        if (res < 0) {
            // Cancel update if store_limit fails
            queue_limits_cancel_update(q);
            return res;
        }
        // Commit update safely using queue_limits_commit_update_frozen
        res = queue_limits_commit_update_frozen(q, &lim);
        if (res)
            return res;
        return length;
    }

    // Handle store with locking and freezing
    mutex_lock(&q->sysfs_lock);
    blk_mq_freeze_queue(q);
    res = entry->store(disk, page, length);
    blk_mq_unfreeze_queue(q);
    mutex_unlock(&q->sysfs_lock);

    return res;
}

The patch does not address the root cause of the vulnerability, which is improper control of the resource lifetime. Specifically, the patch replaces the mutex lock 'udc_lock' with 'udc->connect_lock', but this does not resolve the underlying issue of ensuring proper synchronization and safe resource cleanup. The patch also introduces potential inconsistencies by modifying the locking mechanism without ensuring that all related code paths are updated accordingly.
The patch changes the mutex used for locking from 'udc_lock' to 'udc->connect_lock' but does not introduce any new checks or mechanisms to ensure proper control of the resource's lifetime or prevent use-after-free scenarios.
  • The patch does not address the improper control of the resource's lifetime, leaving the vulnerability unmitigated.
  • Changing the mutex without updating all related code paths may introduce new race conditions or inconsistencies.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
void usb_del_gadget(struct usb_gadget *gadget)
{
	struct usb_udc *udc = gadget->udc;

	if (!udc)
		return;

	dev_vdbg(gadget->dev.parent, "unregistering gadget\n");

	mutex_lock(&udc_lock);
	list_del(&udc->list);
	mutex_unlock(&udc_lock);

	kobject_uevent(&udc->dev.kobj, KOBJ_REMOVE);
	sysfs_remove_link(&udc->dev.kobj, "gadget");
	flush_work(&gadget->work);
	device_del(&gadget->dev);
	ida_free(&gadget_id_numbers, gadget->id_number);
	cancel_work_sync(&udc->vbus_work);
	device_unregister(&udc->dev);
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(usb_del_gadget);
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

void usb_del_gadget(struct usb_gadget *gadget)
{
    // Retrieve the USB device controller associated with the gadget
    struct usb_udc *udc = gadget->udc;

    // If there is no associated USB device controller, exit the function
    if (!udc)
        return;

    // Log a debug message indicating the gadget is being unregistered
    dev_vdbg(&gadget->dev, "unregistering gadget\n");

    // Acquire the lock to ensure exclusive access to the USB device controller list
    mutex_lock(&udc->connect_lock);

    // Remove the USB device controller from the list
    list_del(&udc->list);

    // Release the lock after modifying the list
    mutex_unlock(&udc->connect_lock);

    // Notify the system that the USB device controller is being removed
    kobject_uevent(&gadget->dev.kobj, 0);

    // Remove the sysfs link for the gadget
    sysfs_remove_link(&gadget->dev.kobj, "gadget");

    // Ensure any pending work for the gadget is completed
    flush_work(&gadget->work);

    // Remove the device associated with the gadget
    device_del(&gadget->dev);

    // Free the ID number associated with the gadget
    ida_free(&gadget->dev.id, gadget->id_number);

    // Cancel any pending work related to the USB device controller's VBUS
    cancel_work_sync(&udc->vbus_work);

    // Unregister the device associated with the USB device controller
    device_unregister(&udc->dev);
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

void usb_del_gadget(struct usb_gadget *gadget)
{
    // Retrieve the USB device controller associated with the gadget
    struct usb_udc *udc = gadget->udc;

    // If there is no associated USB device controller, exit the function
    if (!udc)
        return;

    // Log a debug message indicating the gadget is being unregistered
    dev_vdbg(&gadget->dev, "unregistering gadget\n");

    // Acquire the lock to ensure exclusive access to the USB device controller list
    mutex_lock(&udc->connect_lock);

    // Remove the USB device controller from the list
    list_del(&udc->list);

    // Release the lock after modifying the list
    mutex_unlock(&udc->connect_lock);

    // Notify the system that the USB device controller is being removed
    kobject_uevent(&gadget->dev.kobj, KOBJ_REMOVE);

    // Remove the sysfs link for the gadget
    sysfs_remove_link(&gadget->dev.kobj, "gadget");

    // Remove the device associated with the gadget
    device_del(&gadget->dev);

    // Ensure any pending work for the gadget is completed
    flush_work(&gadget->work);

    // Free the ID number associated with the gadget
    ida_free(&gadget->dev.id, gadget->id_number);

    // Cancel any pending work related to the USB device controller's VBUS
    cancel_work_sync(&udc->vbus_work);

    // Unregister the device associated with the USB device controller
    device_unregister(&udc->dev);
}

// Export the function to be available for use in other modules
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(usb_del_gadget);

LLM Assessment

NOT_FIXED (confidence: 0.95)
The patch does not address the root cause of the memory leak described in CVE-2025-21841. The original vulnerability stems from the lack of proper cleanup for the `policy` object when the function exits early due to `amd_pstate_prefcore` being disabled. The patch introduces cosmetic changes and reorders some code but fails to ensure that `cpufreq_cpu_put(policy)` is called in all exit paths, leaving the memory leak unresolved.
The patch modifies variable initialization and adds comments but does not introduce the necessary cleanup logic to prevent the memory leak when `policy` is allocated but not released.
  • Memory leak persists due to missing cleanup for `policy` in early exit paths
  • Patch introduces no substantive changes to address the vulnerability

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static void amd_pstate_update_limits(unsigned int cpu)
{
	struct cpufreq_policy *policy = cpufreq_cpu_get(cpu);
	struct amd_cpudata *cpudata;
	u32 prev_high = 0, cur_high = 0;
	int ret;
	bool highest_perf_changed = false;

	if (!policy)
		return;

	cpudata = policy->driver_data;

	if (!amd_pstate_prefcore)
		return;

	guard(mutex)(&amd_pstate_driver_lock);

	ret = amd_get_highest_perf(cpu, &cur_high);
	if (ret)
		goto free_cpufreq_put;

	prev_high = READ_ONCE(cpudata->prefcore_ranking);
	highest_perf_changed = (prev_high != cur_high);
	if (highest_perf_changed) {
		WRITE_ONCE(cpudata->prefcore_ranking, cur_high);

		if (cur_high < CPPC_MAX_PERF)
			sched_set_itmt_core_prio((int)cur_high, cpu);
	}

free_cpufreq_put:
	cpufreq_cpu_put(policy);

	if (!highest_perf_changed)
		cpufreq_update_policy(cpu);

}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static void amd_pstate_update_limits(unsigned int cpu)
{
    struct cpufreq_policy *policy = cpufreq_cpu_get(cpu);
    struct amd_cpudata *cpudata;
    u32 prev_high, cur_high;
    bool highest_perf_changed = false;

    if (!policy)
        return;

    cpudata = (struct amd_cpudata *)policy->driver_data;

    if (!amd_pstate_prefcore)
        return;

    guard(mutex, 0);

    int ret = amd_get_highest_perf(cpu, &cur_high);

    if (ret)
        goto free_cpufreq_put;

    prev_high = READ_ONCE(&cpudata->highest_perf);

    highest_perf_changed = (cur_high != prev_high);

    if (highest_perf_changed) {
        WRITE_ONCE(&cpudata->highest_perf, cur_high);

        if (cur_high < CPPC_MAX_PERF)
            sched_set_itmt_core_prio((int)cur_high, cpu);
    }

free_cpufreq_put:
    cpufreq_cpu_put(policy);

    if (!highest_perf_changed)
        cpufreq_update_policy(cpu);
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

static void amd_pstate_update_limits(unsigned int cpu)
{
    struct cpufreq_policy *policy = NULL;
    struct amd_cpudata *cpudata;
    u32 prev_high = 0, cur_high = 0;
    int ret;
    bool highest_perf_changed = false;

    // Check if preferred core feature is enabled
    if (!amd_pstate_prefcore)
        return;

    // Retrieve the cpufreq policy for the given CPU
    policy = cpufreq_cpu_get(cpu);
    if (!policy)
        return;

    // Retrieve the driver-specific data from the policy
    cpudata = policy->driver_data;

    // Acquire a lock to ensure thread safety
    guard(mutex)(&amd_pstate_driver_lock);

    // Get the current highest performance for the CPU
    ret = amd_get_highest_perf(cpu, &cur_high);
    if (ret)
        goto free_cpufreq_put;

    // Read the previous highest performance value
    prev_high = READ_ONCE(cpudata->highest_perf);

    // Determine if the highest performance has changed
    highest_perf_changed = (cur_high != prev_high);

    // If the highest performance has changed, update the stored value
    if (highest_perf_changed) {
        // Write the new highest performance value
        WRITE_ONCE(cpudata->highest_perf, cur_high);

        // If the current highest performance is below the maximum, update core priority
        if (cur_high < CPPC_MAX_PERF)
            sched_set_itmt_core_prio((int)cur_high, cpu);
    }

free_cpufreq_put:
    // Release the cpufreq policy
    cpufreq_cpu_put(policy);

    // If the highest performance has not changed, update the cpufreq policy
    if (!highest_perf_changed)
        cpufreq_update_policy(cpu);
}

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
static int init_user_queue(struct process_queue_manager *pqm,
				struct kfd_node *dev, struct queue **q,
				struct queue_properties *q_properties,
				unsigned int qid)
{
	int retval;

	/* Doorbell initialized in user space*/
	q_properties->doorbell_ptr = NULL;
	q_properties->exception_status = KFD_EC_MASK(EC_QUEUE_NEW);

	/* let DQM handle it*/
	q_properties->vmid = 0;
	q_properties->queue_id = qid;

	retval = init_queue(q, q_properties);
	if (retval != 0)
		return retval;

	(*q)->device = dev;
	(*q)->process = pqm->process;

	if (dev->kfd->shared_resources.enable_mes) {
		retval = amdgpu_amdkfd_alloc_gtt_mem(dev->adev,
						AMDGPU_MES_GANG_CTX_SIZE,
						&(*q)->gang_ctx_bo,
						&(*q)->gang_ctx_gpu_addr,
						&(*q)->gang_ctx_cpu_ptr,
						false);
		if (retval) {
			pr_err("failed to allocate gang context bo\n");
			goto cleanup;
		}
		memset((*q)->gang_ctx_cpu_ptr, 0, AMDGPU_MES_GANG_CTX_SIZE);

		/* Starting with GFX11, wptr BOs must be mapped to GART for MES to determine work
		 * on unmapped queues for usermode queue oversubscription (no aggregated doorbell)
		 */
		if (((dev->adev->mes.sched_version & AMDGPU_MES_API_VERSION_MASK)
		    >> AMDGPU_MES_API_VERSION_SHIFT) >= 2) {
			if (dev->adev != amdgpu_ttm_adev(q_properties->wptr_bo->tbo.bdev)) {
				pr_err("Queue memory allocated to wrong device\n");
				retval = -EINVAL;
				goto free_gang_ctx_bo;
			}

			retval = amdgpu_amdkfd_map_gtt_bo_to_gart(q_properties->wptr_bo,
								  &(*q)->wptr_bo_gart);
			if (retval) {
				pr_err("Failed to map wptr bo to GART\n");
				goto free_gang_ctx_bo;
			}
		}
	}

	pr_debug("PQM After init queue");
	return 0;

free_gang_ctx_bo:
	amdgpu_amdkfd_free_gtt_mem(dev->adev, (*q)->gang_ctx_bo);
cleanup:
	uninit_queue(*q);
	*q = NULL;
	return retval;
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

static int init_user_queue(struct process_queue_manager *pqm,
                           struct kfd_node *dev, struct queue **q,
                           struct queue_properties *q_properties,
                           unsigned int qid) {
    int retval;

    q_properties->doorbell_ptr = NULL;
    q_properties->exception_status = 0;
    q_properties->vmid = 0;
    q_properties->queue_id = qid;

    retval = init_queue(q, q_properties);
    if (retval != 0)
        return retval;

    (*q)->device = dev;
    (*q)->process = pqm->process;

    if (dev->kfd->shared_resources.enable_mes) {
        retval = amdgpu_amdkfd_alloc_gtt_mem(dev->adev,
                   0,
                   &(*q)->gang_ctx_bo,
                   &(*q)->gang_ctx_gpu_addr,
                   &(*q)->gang_ctx_cpu_ptr,
                   false);
        if (retval) {
            pr_err("failed to allocate gang context bo\n");
            goto cleanup;
        }

        memset((*q)->gang_ctx_cpu_ptr, 0, 0);
        
        if (((dev->adev->mes.sched_version & 0)
            >> 0) >= 2) {
            if (dev->adev != amdgpu_ttm_adev(q_properties->wptr_bo->tbo.bdev)) {
                pr_err("Queue memory allocated to wrong device\n");
                retval = -1;
                goto free_gang_ctx_bo;
            }

            retval = amdgpu_amdkfd_map_gtt_bo_to_gart(q_properties->wptr_bo,
                                                      &(*q)->wptr_bo_gart);
            if (retval) {
                pr_err("Failed to map wptr bo to GART\n");
                goto free_gang_ctx_bo;
            }
        }
    }

    pr_debug("PQM After init queue");
    return 0;

free_gang_ctx_bo:
    amdgpu_amdkfd_free_gtt_mem(dev->adev, (*q)->gang_ctx_bo);
cleanup:
    uninit_queue(*q);
    *q = NULL;
    return retval;
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

static int init_user_queue(struct process_queue_manager *pqm,
struct kfd_node *dev, struct queue **q,
struct queue_properties *q_properties,
unsigned int qid)
{
    int retval;

    // Initialize the doorbell pointer to NULL as it is set in user space
    q_properties->doorbell_ptr = NULL;

    // Set the exception status to indicate a new queue
    q_properties->exception_status = KFD_EC_MASK(EC_QUEUE_NEW);

    // Set the VMID to 0 to let the Device Queue Manager handle it
    q_properties->vmid = 0;

    // Assign the queue ID to the queue properties
    q_properties->queue_id = qid;

    // Initialize the queue with the given properties
    retval = init_queue(q, q_properties);
    if (retval != 0)
        return retval;

    // Set the device for the queue
    (*q)->device = dev;

    // Set the process for the queue
    (*q)->process = pqm->process;

    // Check if MES (Multi-Engine Scheduler) is enabled in shared resources
    if (dev->kfd->shared_resources.enable_mes) {
        // Allocate GTT memory for the gang context buffer object
        retval = amdgpu_amdkfd_alloc_gtt_mem(dev->adev,
                                             AMDGPU_MES_GANG_CTX_SIZE,
                                             &(*q)->gang_ctx_bo,
                                             &(*q)->gang_ctx_gpu_addr,
                                             &(*q)->gang_ctx_cpu_ptr,
                                             false);
        if (retval) {
            pr_err("failed to allocate gang context bo\n");
            goto cleanup;
        }

        // Initialize the gang context memory to zero
        memset((*q)->gang_ctx_cpu_ptr, 0, AMDGPU_MES_GANG_CTX_SIZE);

        // Check if the MES API version is 2 or higher
        if (((dev->adev->mes.sched_version & AMDGPU_MES_API_VERSION_MASK)
             >> AMDGPU_MES_API_VERSION_SHIFT) >= 2) {
            // Verify that the queue memory is allocated to the correct device
            if (dev->adev != amdgpu_ttm_adev(q_properties->wptr_bo->tbo.bdev)) {
                pr_err("Queue memory allocated to wrong device\n");
                retval = -EINVAL;
                goto free_gang_ctx_bo;
            }

            // Map the write pointer buffer object to GART
            retval = amdgpu_amdkfd_map_gtt_bo_to_gart(q_properties->wptr_bo,
                                                      &(*q)->wptr_bo_gart);
            if (retval) {
                pr_err("Failed to map wptr bo to GART\n");
                goto free_gang_ctx_bo;
            }
        }
    }

    // Log a debug message after initializing the queue
    pr_debug("PQM After init queue");

    // Return success
    return 0;

free_gang_ctx_bo:
    // Free the gang context buffer object if mapping fails
    amdgpu_amdkfd_free_gtt_mem(dev->adev, &(*q)->gang_ctx_bo);

cleanup:
    // Uninitialize the queue if any error occurs
    uninit_queue(*q);

    // Set the queue pointer to NULL
    *q = NULL;

    // Return the error code
    return retval;
}

LLM Assessment

FIXED (confidence: 0.95)
The patch addresses the root cause of the vulnerability by initializing the 'result' variable to 0 at the start of the function, ensuring it is not used uninitialized in the error handling path. This directly mitigates the CWE-457 (Use of Uninitialized Variable) issue described in CVE-2025-21987. The rest of the function logic remains intact, and no new vulnerabilities are introduced.
The patch initializes the 'result' variable to 0 at the start of the function, ensuring it has a defined value even if the function exits early or encounters an error before 'result' is explicitly set.

Input (Vulnerable Code)

```c
int amdgpu_ttm_clear_buffer(struct amdgpu_bo *bo, struct dma_resv *resv, struct dma_fence **fence)
{
	struct amdgpu_device *adev = amdgpu_ttm_adev(bo->tbo.bdev);
	struct amdgpu_ring *ring = adev->mman.buffer_funcs_ring;
	struct amdgpu_res_cursor cursor;
	u64 addr;
	int r;

	if (!adev->mman.buffer_funcs_enabled)
		return -EINVAL;

	if (!fence)
		return -EINVAL;

	*fence = dma_fence_get_stub();

	amdgpu_res_first(bo->tbo.resource, 0, amdgpu_bo_size(bo), &cursor);

	mutex_lock(&adev->mman.gtt_window_lock);
	while (cursor.remaining) {
		struct dma_fence *next = NULL;
		u64 size;

		if (amdgpu_res_cleared(&cursor)) {
			amdgpu_res_next(&cursor, cursor.size);
			continue;
		}

		/* Never clear more than 256MiB at once to avoid timeouts */
		size = min(cursor.size, 256ULL << 20);

		r = amdgpu_ttm_map_buffer(&bo->tbo, bo->tbo.resource, &cursor,
					  1, ring, false, &size, &addr);
		if (r)
			goto err;

		r = amdgpu_ttm_fill_mem(ring, 0, addr, size, resv,
					&next, true, true);
		if (r)
			goto err;

		dma_fence_put(*fence);
		*fence = next;

		amdgpu_res_next(&cursor, size);
	}
err:
	mutex_unlock(&adev->mman.gtt_window_lock);

	return r;
}
```

Ground Truth (Fixed)

int amdgpu_ttm_clear_buffer(struct amdgpu_bo *bo, struct dma_resv *resv, struct dma_fence **lock)
{
    struct amdgpu_device *segment = amdgpu_ttm_adev(bo->tbo.bdev);
    struct amdgpu_ring *index = segment->mman.buffer_funcs_ring;
    struct amdgpu_res_cursor session;
    u64 address;
    int result = 0;

    if (!segment->mman.buffer_funcs_enabled)
        return -EINVAL;

    if (!lock)
        return -EINVAL;

    *lock = dma_fence_get_stub();

    amdgpu_res_first(bo->tbo.resource, 0, amdgpu_bo_size(bo), &session);

    mutex_lock(&segment->mman.gtt_window_lock);
    while (session.remaining) {
        struct dma_fence *next = NULL;
        u64 length;

        if (amdgpu_res_cleared(&session)) {
            amdgpu_res_next(&session, session.size);
            continue;
        }

        /* Never clear more than 256MiB at once to avoid timeouts */
        length = min(session.size, 256ULL << 20);

        result = amdgpu_ttm_map_buffer(&bo->tbo, bo->tbo.resource, &session,
                      1, index, false, &length, &address);
        if (result)
            goto err;

        result = amdgpu_ttm_fill_mem(index, 0, address, length, resv,
                    &next, true, true);
        if (result)
            goto err;

        dma_fence_put(*lock);
        *lock = next;

        amdgpu_res_next(&session, length);
    }
err:
    mutex_unlock(&segment->mman.gtt_window_lock);

    if (0) {  // Error path was using MALLOC without a definition or purpose
        char fake_buffer[100];
    }

    return result;
}

Agent Patch (Generated)

#define MALLOC false
int amdgpu_ttm_clear_buffer(struct amdgpu_bo *bo, struct dma_resv *resv, struct dma_fence **lock)
{
    struct amdgpu_device *segment = amdgpu_ttm_adev(bo->tbo.bdev);
    struct amdgpu_ring *index = segment->mman.buffer_funcs_ring;
    struct amdgpu_res_cursor session;
    u64 address;
    int result = 0; // Initialize result to 0

    if (!segment->mman.buffer_funcs_enabled)
        return -EINVAL;

    if (!lock)
        return -EINVAL;

    *lock = dma_fence_get_stub();
    amdgpu_res_first(bo->tbo.resource, 0, amdgpu_bo_size(bo), &session);

    mutex_lock(&segment->mman.gtt_window_lock);

    while (session.remaining) {
        struct dma_fence *next = NULL;
        u64 length;

        if (amdgpu_res_cleared(&session)) {
            amdgpu_res_next(&session, session.size);
            continue;
        }

        /* Never clear more than 256MiB at once to avoid timeouts */
        length = min(session.size, 256ULL << 20);

        result = amdgpu_ttm_map_buffer(&bo->tbo, bo->tbo.resource, &session,
                                       1, index, false, &length, &address);
        if (result)
            goto err;

        result = amdgpu_ttm_fill_mem(index, 0, address, length, resv,
                                     &next, true, true);
        if (result)
            goto err;

        dma_fence_put(*lock);
        *lock = next;
        amdgpu_res_next(&session, length);
    }

err:
    mutex_unlock(&segment->mman.gtt_window_lock);

    // Remove the vulnerable MALLOC block
    return result;
}

Augmented Data Pairs

455 pairs. Click to expand. First 5 open by default.

#1 CVE-2022-49733 / augmented   Race Condition

Vulnerable Code

```c
static int snd_pcm_oss_sync(struct snd_pcm_oss_file *pcm_oss_file)
{
	int err = 0;
	unsigned int saved_f_flags;
	struct snd_pcm_substream *substream;
	struct snd_pcm_runtime *runtime;
	snd_pcm_format_t format;
	unsigned long width;
	size_t size;

	substream = pcm_oss_file->streams[SNDRV_PCM_STREAM_PLAYBACK];
	if (substream != NULL) {
		runtime = substream->runtime;
		if (atomic_read(&substream->mmap_count))
			goto __direct;
		if ((err = snd_pcm_oss_make_ready(substream)) < 0)
			return err;
		atomic_inc(&runtime->oss.rw_ref);
		if (mutex_lock_interruptible(&runtime->oss.params_lock)) {
			atomic_dec(&runtime->oss.rw_ref);
			return -ERESTARTSYS;
		}
		format = snd_pcm_oss_format_from(runtime->oss.format);
		width = snd_pcm_format_physical_width(format);
		if (runtime->oss.buffer_used > 0) {
#ifdef OSS_DEBUG
			pcm_dbg(substream->pcm, "sync: buffer_used\n");
#endif
			size = (8 * (runtime->oss.period_bytes - runtime->oss.buffer_used) + 7) / width;
			snd_pcm_format_set_silence(format,
						   runtime->oss.buffer + runtime->oss.buffer_used,
						   size);
			err = snd_pcm_oss_sync1(substream, runtime->oss.period_bytes);
			if (err < 0)
				goto unlock;
		} else if (runtime->oss.period_ptr > 0) {
#ifdef OSS_DEBUG
			pcm_dbg(substream->pcm, "sync: period_ptr\n");
#endif
			size = runtime->oss.period_bytes - runtime->oss.period_ptr;
			snd_pcm_format_set_silence(format,
						   runtime->oss.buffer,
						   size * 8 / width);
			err = snd_pcm_oss_sync1(substream, size);
			if (err < 0)
				goto unlock;
		}
		/*
		 * The ALSA's period might be a bit large than OSS one.
		 * Fill the remain portion of ALSA period with zeros.
		 */
		size = runtime->control->appl_ptr % runtime->period_size;
		if (size > 0) {
			size = runtime->period_size - size;
			if (runtime->access == SNDRV_PCM_ACCESS_RW_INTERLEAVED)
				snd_pcm_lib_write(substream, NULL, size);
			else if (runtime->access == SNDRV_PCM_ACCESS_RW_NONINTERLEAVED)
				snd_pcm_lib_writev(substream, NULL, size);
		}
unlock:
		mutex_unlock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
		atomic_dec(&runtime->oss.rw_ref);
		if (err < 0)
			return err;
		/*
		 * finish sync: drain the buffer
		 */
	      __direct:
		saved_f_flags = substream->f_flags;
		substream->f_flags &= ~O_NONBLOCK;
		err = snd_pcm_kernel_ioctl(substream, SNDRV_PCM_IOCTL_DRAIN, NULL);
		substream->f_flags = saved_f_flags;
		if (err < 0)
			return err;
		mutex_lock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
		runtime->oss.prepare = 1;
		mutex_unlock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
	}

	substream = pcm_oss_file->streams[SNDRV_PCM_STREAM_CAPTURE];
	if (substream != NULL) {
		if ((err = snd_pcm_oss_make_ready(substream)) < 0)
			return err;
		runtime = substream->runtime;
		err = snd_pcm_kernel_ioctl(substream, SNDRV_PCM_IOCTL_DROP, NULL);
		if (err < 0)
			return err;
		mutex_lock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
		runtime->oss.buffer_used = 0;
		runtime->oss.prepare = 1;
		mutex_unlock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
	}
	return 0;
}
```

Ground Truth (augmented)

static int snd_pcm_oss_sync(struct snd_pcm_oss_file *pcm_oss_file)
{
    int balance = 0;
    unsigned int saved_f_flags;
    struct snd_pcm_substream *substream;
    struct snd_pcm_runtime *runtime;
    snd_pcm_format_t format;
    unsigned long checksum;
    unsigned long buffer_length;

    substream = pcm_oss_file->streams[SNDRV_PCM_STREAM_PLAYBACK];
    if (substream != (struct snd_pcm_substream *)0) {
        runtime = substream->runtime;
        if (atomic_read(&substream->runtime->oss.rw_ref))
            goto __direct;
        if ((balance = snd_pcm_oss_make_ready(substream)) < 0)
            return balance;
        atomic_inc(&runtime->oss.rw_ref);
        if (mutex_lock_interruptible(&runtime->oss.params_lock)) {
            atomic_dec(&runtime->oss.rw_ref);
            return -ERESTARTSYS;
        }
        format = snd_pcm_oss_format_from(runtime->oss.format);
        checksum = snd_pcm_format_physical_width(format);
        if (runtime->oss.buffer_used > 0) {
#ifdef OSS_DEBUG
            // pcm_dbg(substream->pcm, "sync: buffer_used\n");
#endif
            buffer_length = (8 * (runtime->oss.period_bytes - runtime->oss.buffer_used) + 7) / checksum;
            snd_pcm_format_set_silence(format,
                                       runtime->oss.buffer + runtime->oss.buffer_used,
                                       buffer_length);
            balance = snd_pcm_oss_sync1(substream, runtime->oss.period_bytes);
            if (balance < 0)
                goto unlock;
        } else if (runtime->oss.period_ptr > 0) {
#ifdef OSS_DEBUG
            // pcm_dbg(substream->pcm, "sync: period_ptr\n");
#endif
            buffer_length = runtime->oss.period_bytes - runtime->oss.period_ptr;
            snd_pcm_format_set_silence(format,
                                       runtime->oss.buffer,
                                       buffer_length * 8 / checksum);
            balance = snd_pcm_oss_sync1(substream, buffer_length);
            if (balance < 0)
                goto unlock;
        }
        buffer_length = runtime->control->appl_ptr % runtime->period_size;
        if (buffer_length > 0) {
            buffer_length = runtime->period_size - buffer_length;
            if (runtime->access == SNDRV_PCM_ACCESS_RW_INTERLEAVED)
                snd_pcm_lib_write(substream, (char *)0, buffer_length);
            else if (runtime->access == SNDRV_PCM_ACCESS_RW_NONINTERLEAVED)
                snd_pcm_lib_writev(substream, (char *)0, buffer_length);
        }
unlock:
        mutex_unlock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
        atomic_dec(&runtime->oss.rw_ref);
        if (balance < 0)
            return balance;
      __direct:
        saved_f_flags = substream->f_flags;
        substream->f_flags &= ~O_NONBLOCK;
        balance = snd_pcm_kernel_ioctl(substream, SNDRV_PCM_IOCTL_DRAIN, (void *)0);
        substream->f_flags = saved_f_flags;
        if (balance < 0)
            return balance;
        mutex_lock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
        runtime->oss.prepare = 1;
        mutex_unlock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
    }

    substream = pcm_oss_file->streams[SNDRV_PCM_STREAM_CAPTURE];
    if (substream != (struct snd_pcm_substream *)0) {
        if ((balance = snd_pcm_oss_make_ready(substream)) < 0)
            return balance;
        runtime = substream->runtime;
        balance = snd_pcm_kernel_ioctl(substream, SNDRV_PCM_IOCTL_DROP, (void *)0);
        if (balance < 0)
            return balance;
        mutex_lock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
        runtime->oss.buffer_used = 0;
        runtime->oss.prepare = 1;
        mutex_unlock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
    }
    return 0;
}

static int snd_pcm_oss_sync(struct snd_pcm_oss_file *pcm_oss_file)
{
	int err = 0;
	unsigned int saved_f_flags;
	struct snd_pcm_substream *substream;
	struct snd_pcm_runtime *runtime;
	snd_pcm_format_t format;
	unsigned long width;
	size_t size;

	substream = pcm_oss_file->streams[SNDRV_PCM_STREAM_PLAYBACK];
	if (substream != NULL) {
		runtime = substream->runtime;
		if (atomic_read(&substream->mmap_count))
			goto __direct;
		if ((err = snd_pcm_oss_make_ready(substream)) < 0)
			return err;
		atomic_inc(&runtime->oss.rw_ref);
		if (mutex_lock_interruptible(&runtime->oss.params_lock)) {
			atomic_dec(&runtime->oss.rw_ref);
			return -ERESTARTSYS;
		}
		format = snd_pcm_oss_format_from(runtime->oss.format);
		width = snd_pcm_format_physical_width(format);
		if (runtime->oss.buffer_used > 0) {
#ifdef OSS_DEBUG
			pcm_dbg(substream->pcm, "sync: buffer_used\n");
#endif
			size = (8 * (runtime->oss.period_bytes - runtime->oss.buffer_used) + 7) / width;
			snd_pcm_format_set_silence(format,
						   runtime->oss.buffer + runtime->oss.buffer_used,
						   size);
			err = snd_pcm_oss_sync1(substream, runtime->oss.period_bytes);
			if (err < 0)
				goto unlock;
		} else if (runtime->oss.period_ptr > 0) {
#ifdef OSS_DEBUG
			pcm_dbg(substream->pcm, "sync: period_ptr\n");
#endif
			size = runtime->oss.period_bytes - runtime->oss.period_ptr;
			snd_pcm_format_set_silence(format,
						   runtime->oss.buffer,
						   size * 8 / width);
			err = snd_pcm_oss_sync1(substream, size);
			if (err < 0)
				goto unlock;
		}
		/*
		 * The ALSA's period might be a bit large than OSS one.
		 * Fill the remain portion of ALSA period with zeros.
		 */
		size = runtime->control->appl_ptr % runtime->period_size;
		if (size > 0) {
			size = runtime->period_size - size;
			if (runtime->access == SNDRV_PCM_ACCESS_RW_INTERLEAVED)
				snd_pcm_lib_write(substream, NULL, size);
			else if (runtime->access == SNDRV_PCM_ACCESS_RW_NONINTERLEAVED)
				snd_pcm_lib_writev(substream, NULL, size);
		}
unlock:
		mutex_unlock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
		atomic_dec(&runtime->oss.rw_ref);
		if (err < 0)
			return err;
		/*
		 * finish sync: drain the buffer
		 */
	      __direct:
		saved_f_flags = substream->f_flags;
		substream->f_flags &= ~O_NONBLOCK;
		err = snd_pcm_kernel_ioctl(substream, SNDRV_PCM_IOCTL_DRAIN, NULL);
		substream->f_flags = saved_f_flags;
		if (err < 0)
			return err;
		mutex_lock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
		runtime->oss.prepare = 1;
		mutex_unlock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
	}

	substream = pcm_oss_file->streams[SNDRV_PCM_STREAM_CAPTURE];
	if (substream != NULL) {
		if ((err = snd_pcm_oss_make_ready(substream)) < 0)
			return err;
		runtime = substream->runtime;
		err = snd_pcm_kernel_ioctl(substream, SNDRV_PCM_IOCTL_DROP, NULL);
		if (err < 0)
			return err;
		mutex_lock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
		runtime->oss.buffer_used = 0;
		runtime->oss.prepare = 1;
		mutex_unlock(&runtime->oss.params_lock);
	}
	return 0;
}
